speaker: NS

words: a church,you know. And that church, of course, is demolished, you know. It

words: Here near Moscow, in this county, you know. So they made up their mind that

words: next Sunday, you know, they were going to kill him. They knew just which

words: fine all the rest of the way. Well, you know, he used to tell so many different

words: Lord, our God in vain? Why, that's terrible." That man std,"Who are you? What

words: Presbyterian. And I found that the only difference—the faith is the same—

words: the only difference is the way they do things. Now they take communion in

speaker: NS
words: a different way and things like that, that's all. I canft see any difference,

speaker: NS
words: only just the way they do things.

speaker: LS
words: church. Did they have a better Sunday school teacher or. . .?

speaker: NS
words: The catechism, we had to memorize psalms, you know, if you know what that is.

speaker: NS
words: Ananthe Presbyterian church we had to take a training course so it really

speaker: NS
words: wasrtt any different, only we weren't confirmed. In the Lutheran church

speaker: NS
words: they call it Confirmation, you know. They had a Sunday when they call it

words: that they confirmed. They ask them questions and the children answer,

speaker: NS
words: you know. But we don't do that in the Presbyterian church. We don't have so

words: much catechism, that's all. But now the way they take communion is a liitle

words: different. That's all together different. Now you know whnn I went to the

speaker: NS

words: altar. And the minister had a little—well, I'd say a container, I'd don't

speaker: NS
words: know what you'd call it—and it's supposed to be the bread and wine, you know.

words: And he'd give each one a piece of that. It was just like a mint, you know,

speaker: NS
words: and go on to the next one. And then he'd give each one a sip of the wine and

speaker: NS
words: go on to the next one. Well, now the Presbyterians don't do it that

speaker: NS
words: way at all. All they do is you sit in your seats and they pass the communion

speaker: NS
words: araind to you, that's all. It means the same, just a different way, you know.

words: know. But I remember when, you see, his wife,my grandmother died when she

words: Laura Schrager.

words: teach again and leave the children with my mother. Oh, how I hated to leave
